In Year 10 English, under Literacy (Interpreting, analysing, evaluating), students learn to: Choose a reading technique and reading path appropriate for the type of text, to retrieve and connect ideas within and between texts

In Year 10 English, under Literacy (Interpreting, analysing, evaluating), students learn to: Use comprehension strategies to compare and contrast information within and between texts, identifying and analysing embedded perspectives, and evaluating supporting evidence

In Year 10 English, under Literacy (Creating texts), students learn to: Create sustained texts, including texts that combine specific digital or media content, for imaginative, informative, or persuasive purposes that reflect upon challenging and complex issues

In Year 10 English, under Literacy (Creating texts), students learn to: Review, edit and refine students’ own and others’ texts for control of content, organisation, sentence structure, vocabulary, and/or visual features to achieve particular purposes and effects

In Year 10 English, under Literacy (Creating texts), students learn to: Use a range of software, including word processing programs, confidently, flexibly and imaginatively to create, edit and publish texts, considering the identified purpose and the characteristics of the user

In Year 1 English, under Language (Language variation and change), students learn to: Understand that English is one of many languages spoken in Australia and that different languages may be spoken by family, classmates and community
In Year 1 English, under Language (Language for interaction), students learn to: Explore how language is used differently at home and school depending on the relationships between people
In Year 1 English, under Language (Language for interaction), students learn to: Understand that language can be used to explore ways of expressing needs, likes and dislikes

In Year 1 English, under Language (Text structure and organisation), students learn to: Understand that texts can take many forms, can be very short (for example an exit sign) or quite long (for example an information book or a film) and that stories and informative texts have different purposes
In Year 1 English, under Language (Text structure and organisation), students learn to: Understand that some language in written texts is unlike everyday spoken language

In Year 1 English, under Language (Text structure and organisation), students learn to: Understand that punctuation is a feature of written text different from letters; recognise how capital letters are used for names, and that capital letters and full stops signal the beginning and end of sentences

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Language variation and change), students learn to: Understand that people use different systems of communication to cater to different needs and purposes and that many people may use sign systems to communicate with others

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Language for interaction), students learn to: Understand that language is used in combination with other means of communication, for example facial expressions and gestures to interact with others
In Foundation Year English, under Language (Language for interaction), students learn to: Understand that there are different ways of asking for information, making offers and giving commands
In Foundation Year English, under Language (Language for interaction), students learn to: Explore different ways of expressing emotions, including verbal, visual, body language and facial expressions
In Foundation Year English, under Language (Text structure and organisation), students learn to: Understand that the purposes texts serve shape their structure in predictable ways
In Foundation Year English, under Language (Text structure and organisation), students learn to: Understand patterns of repetition and contrast in simple texts

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Text structure and organisation), students learn to: Recognise that different types of punctuation, including full stops, question marks and exclamation marks, signal sentences that make statements, ask questions, express emotion or give commands

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Text structure and organisation), students learn to: Understand concepts about print and screen, including how different types of texts are organised using page numbering, tables of content, headings and titles, navigation buttons, bars and links

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Expressing and developing ideas), students learn to: Identify the parts of a simple sentence that represent ‘What’s happening?’, ‘What state is being described?’, ‘Who or what is involved?’ and the surrounding circumstances

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Expressing and developing ideas), students learn to: Explore differences in words that represent people, places and things (nouns, including pronouns), happenings and states (verbs), qualities (adjectives) and details such as when, where and how (adverbs)

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Expressing and developing ideas), students learn to: Compare different kinds of images in narrative and informative texts and discuss how they contribute to meaning

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Text structure and organisation), students learn to: Understand how texts are made cohesive through language features, including word associations, synonyms, and antonyms
In Foundation Year English, under Language (Text structure and organisation), students learn to: Recognise that capital letters signal proper nouns and commas are used to separate items in lists

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Text structure and organisation), students learn to: Know some features of text organisation including page and screen layouts, alphabetical order, and different types of diagrams, for example timelines

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Expressing and developing ideas), students learn to: Understand that simple connections can be made between ideas by using a compound sentence with two or more clauses usually linked by a coordinating conjunction

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Expressing and developing ideas), students learn to: Understand that nouns represent people, places, concrete objects and abstract concepts; that there are three types of nouns: common, proper and pronouns; and that noun groups/phrases can be expanded using articles and adjectives

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Expressing and developing ideas), students learn to: Identify visual representations of characters’ actions, reactions, speech and thought processes in narratives, and consider how these images add to or contradict or multiply the meaning of accompanying words

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Expressing and developing ideas), students learn to: Understand the use of vocabulary about familiar and new topics and experiment with and begin to make conscious choices of vocabulary to suit audience and purpose

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Phonics and word knowledge), students learn to: Build morphemic word families using knowledge of prefixes and suffixes

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Phonics and word knowledge), students learn to: Use knowledge of letter patterns and morphemes to read and write high-frequency words and words whose spelling is not predictable from their sounds

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Language variation and change), students learn to: Understand that Standard Australian English is one of many social dialects used in Australia, and that while it originated in England it has been influenced by many other languages

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Language for interaction), students learn to: Understand that social interactions influence the way people engage with ideas and respond to others for example when exploring and clarifying the ideas of others, summarising their own views and reporting them to a larger group

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Language for interaction), students learn to: Understand differences between the language of opinion and feeling and the language of factual reporting or recording

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Text structure and organisation), students learn to: Understand how texts vary in complexity and technicality depending on the approach to the topic, the purpose and the intended audience

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Text structure and organisation), students learn to: Understand how texts are made cohesive through the use of linking devices including pronoun reference and text connectives
In Foundation Year English, under Language (Text structure and organisation), students learn to: Recognise how quotation marks are used in texts to signal dialogue, titles and quoted (direct) speech

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Text structure and organisation), students learn to: Identify features of online texts that enhance readability including text, navigation, links, graphics and layout

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Expressing and developing ideas), students learn to: Understand that the meaning of sentences can be enriched through the use of noun groups/phrases and verb groups/phrases and prepositional phrases
In Foundation Year English, under Language (Expressing and developing ideas), students learn to: Investigate how quoted (direct) and reported (indirect) speech work in different types of text

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Expressing and developing ideas), students learn to: Understand how adverb groups/phrases and prepositional phrases work in different ways to provide circumstantial details about an activity

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Expressing and developing ideas), students learn to: Explore the effect of choices when framing an image, placement of elements in the image, and salience on composition of still and moving images in a range of types of texts

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Expressing and developing ideas), students learn to: Incorporate new vocabulary from a range of sources into students’ own texts including vocabulary encountered in research

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Phonics and word knowledge), students learn to: Understand how to use knowledge of letter patterns including double letters, spelling generalisations, morphemic word families, common prefixes and suffixes and word origins to spell more complex words

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Phonics and word knowledge), students learn to: Read and write a large core of high frequency words including homophones and know how to use context to identify correct spelling

In Foundation Year English, under Language (Phonics and word knowledge), students learn to: Understand how to use phonic knowledge to read and write multisyllabic words with more complex letter combinations, including a variety of vowel sounds and known prefixes and suffixes

In Year 1 English, under Literature (Literature and context), students learn to: Recognise that texts are created by authors who tell stories and share experiences that may be similar or different to students’ own experiences
In Year 1 English, under Literature (Responding to literature), students learn to: Respond to texts, identifying favourite stories, authors and illustrators
In Year 1 English, under Literature (Responding to literature), students learn to: Share feelings and thoughts about the events and characters in texts

In Year 1 English, under Literature (Examining literature), students learn to: Replicate the rhythms and sound patterns in stories, rhymes, songs and poems from a range of cultures
In Year 1 English, under Literature (Creating literature), students learn to: Retell familiar literary texts through performance, use of illustrations and images
In Year 1 English, under Literature (Creating literature), students learn to: Innovate on familiar texts through play

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Literature and context), students learn to: Identify and explore ideas and viewpoints about events, issues and characters represented in texts drawn from different historical, social and cultural contexts

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Responding to literature), students learn to: Reflect on ideas and opinions about characters, settings and events in literary texts, identifying areas of agreement and difference with others and justifying a point of view

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Responding to literature), students learn to: Compare the ways that language and images are used to create character, and to influence emotions and opinions in different types of texts

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Responding to literature), students learn to: Discuss aspects of texts, for example their aesthetic and social value, using relevant and appropriate metalanguage

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Examining literature), students learn to: Recognise and analyse the ways that characterisation, events and settings are combined in narratives, and discuss the purposes and appeal of different approaches

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Examining literature), students learn to: Understand, interpret and discuss how language is compressed to produce a dramatic effect in film or drama, and to create layers of meaning in poetry, for example haiku, tankas, couplets, free verse and verse novels

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Creating literature), students learn to: Create literary texts that adapt stylistic features encountered in other texts, for example, narrative viewpoint, structure of stanzas, contrast and juxtaposition

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Creating literature), students learn to: Experiment with text structures and language features and their effects in creating literary texts, for example, using rhythm, sound effects, monologue, layout, navigation and colour

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Literature and context), students learn to: Explore the ways that ideas and viewpoints in literary texts drawn from different historical, social and cultural contexts may reflect or challenge the values of individuals and groups

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Literature and context), students learn to: Explore the interconnectedness of Country/Place, People, Identity and Culture in texts including those by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander authors

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Examining literature), students learn to: Identify and evaluate devices that create tone, for example humour, wordplay, innuendo and parody in poetry, humorous prose, drama or visual texts

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Creating literature), students learn to: Experiment with particular language features drawn from different types of texts, including combinations of language and visual choices to create new texts

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Literature and context), students learn to: Interpret and compare how representations of people and culture in literary texts are drawn from different historical, social and cultural contexts

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Responding to literature), students learn to: Present an argument about a literary text based on initial impressions and subsequent analysis of the whole text

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Responding to literature), students learn to: Reflect on, discuss and explore notions of literary value and how and why such notions vary according to context

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Responding to literature), students learn to: Explore and reflect on personal understanding of the world and significant human experience gained from interpreting various representations of life matters in texts

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Examining literature), students learn to: Analyse texts from familiar and unfamiliar contexts, and discuss and evaluate their content and the appeal of an individual author’s literary style

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Examining literature), students learn to: Investigate and experiment with the use and effect of extended metaphor, metonymy, allegory, icons, myths and symbolism in texts, for example poetry, short films, graphic novels, and plays on similar themes
In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Examining literature), students learn to: Analyse text structures and language features of literary texts, and make relevant comparisons with other texts

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Creating literature), students learn to: Create literary texts, including hybrid texts, that innovate on aspects of other texts, for example by using parody, allusion and appropriation

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Creating literature), students learn to: Experiment with the ways that language features, image and sound can be adapted in literary texts, for example the effects of stereotypical characters and settings, the playfulness of humour and pun and the use of hyperlink

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Literature and context), students learn to: Compare and evaluate a range of representations of individuals and groups in different historical, social and cultural contexts
In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Responding to literature), students learn to: Reflect on, extend, endorse or refute others’ interpretations of and responses to literature

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Responding to literature), students learn to: Analyse and explain how text structures, language features and visual features of texts and the context in which texts are experienced may influence audience response
In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Responding to literature), students learn to: Evaluate the social, moral and ethical positions represented in texts

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Examining literature), students learn to: Identify, explain and discuss how narrative viewpoint, structure, characterisation and devices including analogy and satire shape different interpretations and responses to a text

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Creating literature), students learn to: Create literary texts that reflect an emerging sense of personal style and evaluate the effectiveness of these texts

In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Creating literature), students learn to: Create literary texts with a sustained ‘voice’, selecting and adapting appropriate text structures, literary devices, language, auditory and visual structures and features for a specific purpose and intended audience
In Foundation Year English, under Literature (Creating literature), students learn to: Create imaginative texts that make relevant thematic and intertextual connections with other texts

In Year 1 English, under Literacy (Texts in context), students learn to: Identify some familiar texts and the contexts in which they are used

In Year 1 English, under Literacy (Interacting with others), students learn to: Deliver short oral presentations to peers
In Year 1 English, under Literacy (Interpreting, analysing, evaluating), students learn to: Identify some differences between imaginative and informative texts

In Year 1 English, under Literacy (Creating texts), students learn to: Create short texts to explore, record and report ideas and events using familiar words and beginning writing knowledge
In Year 1 English, under Literacy (Creating texts), students learn to: Participate in shared editing of students’ own texts for meaning, spelling, capital letters and full stops
In Year 1 English, under Literacy (Creating texts), students learn to: Produce some lower case and upper case letters using learned letter formations
In Year 1 English, under Literacy (Creating texts), students learn to: Construct texts using software including word processing programs

In Year 10 English, under Literacy (Creating texts), students learn to: Re-read student’s own texts and discuss possible changes to improve meaning, spelling and punctuation
In Year 10 English, under Literacy (Creating texts), students learn to: Write using unjoined lower case and upper case letters
In Year 10 English, under Literacy (Creating texts), students learn to: Construct texts that incorporate supporting images using software including word processing programs

In Foundation Year English, under Literacy (Texts in context), students learn to: Analyse and explain how language has evolved over time and how technology and the media have influenced language use and forms of communication

In Foundation Year English, under Literacy (Texts in context), students learn to: Analyse how the construction and interpretation of texts, including media texts, can be influenced by cultural perspectives and other texts

In Foundation Year English, under Literacy (Interacting with others), students learn to: Listen to spoken texts constructed for different purposes, for example to entertain and to persuade, and analyse how language features of these texts position listeners to respond in particular ways

In Foundation Year English, under Literacy (Interacting with others), students learn to: Use interaction skills to present and discuss an idea and to influence and engage an audience by selecting persuasive language, varying voice tone, pitch, and pace, and using elements such as music and sound effects

In Foundation Year English, under Literacy (Interacting with others), students learn to: Plan, rehearse and deliver presentations, selecting and sequencing appropriate content and multimodal elements for aesthetic and playful purposes

In Foundation Year English, under Literacy (Interpreting, analysing, evaluating), students learn to: Apply an expanding vocabulary to read increasingly complex texts with fluency and comprehension

In Foundation Year English, under Literacy (Interpreting, analysing, evaluating), students learn to: Use comprehension strategies to interpret and analyse texts, comparing and evaluating representations of an event, issue, situation or character in different texts

In Foundation Year English, under Literacy (Interpreting, analysing, evaluating), students learn to: Explore and explain the combinations of language and visual choices that authors make to present information, opinions and perspectives in different texts

In Foundation Year English, under Literacy (Creating texts), students learn to: Use a range of software, including word processing programs, flexibly and imaginatively to publish texts

In Year 8 English, under Literacy (Interpreting, analysing, evaluating), students learn to: Use comprehension strategies to interpret and analyse texts, comparing and evaluating representations of an event, issue, situation or character in different texts

In Year 8 English, under Literacy (Interpreting, analysing, evaluating), students learn to: Explore and explain the combinations of language and visual choices that authors make to present information, opinions and perspectives in different texts

In Year 8 English, under Literacy (Creating texts), students learn to: Create imaginative, informative and persuasive texts that present a point of view and advance or illustrate arguments, including texts that integrate visual, print and/or audio features

In Year 8 English, under Literacy (Creating texts), students learn to: Review and edit students’ own and others’ texts to improve clarity and control over content, organisation, paragraphing, sentence structure, vocabulary and audio/visual features
In Year 8 English, under Literacy (Creating texts), students learn to: Use a range of software, including word processing programs, flexibly and imaginatively to publish texts

In Year 10 English, under Literacy (Texts in context), students learn to: Analyse how the construction and interpretation of texts, including media texts, can be influenced by cultural perspectives and other texts

In Year 10 English, under Literacy (Interacting with others), students learn to: Listen to spoken texts constructed for different purposes, for example to entertain and to persuade, and analyse how language features of these texts position listeners to respond in particular ways

In Year 10 English, under Literacy (Interacting with others), students learn to: Use interaction skills to present and discuss an idea and to influence and engage an audience by selecting persuasive language, varying voice tone, pitch, and pace, and using elements such as music and sound effects

In Year 10 English, under Literacy (Interacting with others), students learn to: Plan, rehearse and deliver presentations, selecting and sequencing appropriate content and multimodal elements for aesthetic and playful purposes

In Year 10 English, under Literacy (Interpreting, analysing, evaluating), students learn to: Interpret, analyse and evaluate how different perspectives of issue, event, situation, individuals or groups are constructed to serve specific purposes in texts
In Year 10 English, under Literacy (Interpreting, analysing, evaluating), students learn to: Apply an expanding vocabulary to read increasingly complex texts with fluency and comprehension

In Year 10 English, under Literacy (Interpreting, analysing, evaluating), students learn to: Use comprehension strategies to interpret and analyse texts, comparing and evaluating representations of an event, issue, situation or character in different texts

In Year 10 English, under Literacy (Interpreting, analysing, evaluating), students learn to: Explore and explain the combinations of language and visual choices that authors make to present information, opinions and perspectives in different texts

In Year 10 English, under Literacy (Creating texts), students learn to: Create imaginative, informative and persuasive texts that present a point of view and advance or illustrate arguments, including texts that integrate visual, print and/or audio features

In Year 10 English, under Literacy (Creating texts), students learn to: Review and edit students’ own and others’ texts to improve clarity and control over content, organisation, paragraphing, sentence structure, vocabulary and audio/visual features
In Year 10 English, under Literacy (Creating texts), students learn to: Use a range of software, including word processing programs, flexibly and imaginatively to publish texts

In Foundation Year English, under Literacy (Texts in context), students learn to: Analyse and evaluate how people, cultures, places, events, objects and concepts are represented in texts, including media texts, through language, structural and/or visual choices

In Foundation Year English, under Literacy (Interacting with others), students learn to: Identify and explore the purposes and effects of different text structures and language features of spoken texts, and use this knowledge to create purposeful texts that inform, persuade and engage

In Foundation Year English, under Literacy (Interacting with others), students learn to: Use organisation patterns, voice and language conventions to present a point of view on a subject, speaking clearly, coherently and with effect, using logic, imagery and rhetorical devices to engage audiences

In Foundation Year English, under Literacy (Interacting with others), students learn to: Plan, rehearse and deliver presentations, selecting and sequencing appropriate content and multimodal elements to influence a course of action

In Foundation Year English, under Literacy (Interpreting, analysing, evaluating), students learn to: Identify and analyse implicit or explicit values, beliefs and assumptions in texts and how these are influenced by purposes and likely audiences

In Foundation Year English, under Literacy (Interpreting, analysing, evaluating), students learn to: Choose a reading technique and reading path appropriate for the type of text, to retrieve and connect ideas within and between texts

In Foundation Year English, under Literacy (Interpreting, analysing, evaluating), students learn to: Use comprehension strategies to compare and contrast information within and between texts, identifying and analysing embedded perspectives, and evaluating supporting evidence

In Foundation Year English, under Literacy (Creating texts), students learn to: Create sustained texts, including texts that combine specific digital or media content, for imaginative, informative, or persuasive purposes that reflect upon challenging and complex issues

In Foundation Year English, under Literacy (Creating texts), students learn to: Review, edit and refine students’ own and others’ texts for control of content, organisation, sentence structure, vocabulary, and/or visual features to achieve particular purposes and effects

In Foundation Year English, under Literacy (Creating texts), students learn to: Use a range of software, including word processing programs, confidently, flexibly and imaginatively to create, edit and publish texts, considering the identified purpose and the characteristics of the user
